title: Ballard Power Systems Announces 30%+ Restructuring Plan
text: Ballard Power Systems announced a comprehensive global restructuring designed to cut total operating expenses by more than 30% in response to delays in hydrogen infrastructure development and slower-than-expected fuel cell market adoption. CEO Randy MacEwen stated: we see a multi-year push-out of the availability of low-cost, low carbon hydrogen and hydrogen refueling infrastructure. The restructuring includes workforce reductions across global operations, rationalization of product development programs, operational consolidation efforts, reduced capital expenditures, and working capital improvement initiatives. The company projects annualized savings exceeding 30%, with substantial realization expected in 2025. These cuts will not affect product delivery or customer commitments. The Texas Manufacturing Facility expansion, which includes $94 million in U.S. government funding, is being reassessed with plans to review financing options and delay cash outlays pending clearer market adoption signals. The company is conducting a strategic review of its China operations, including all options for its Weichai Ballard joint venture, which has underperformed. Leadership changes include Kate Igbalode replacing Paul Dobson as Chief Financial Officer (effective immediately) and Lee Sweetland succeeding Mark Biznek as Chief Operating Officer (end of 2024). Both departing executives will support their successors during transition periods.
